name: git-protocol
description: Git protocol implementation patterns using gitoxide for Guts repository operations
capabilities: Open and create Git repositories using gitoxide
Read and write Git objects (blobs, trees, commits, tags)
Implement Smart HTTP protocol endpoints for git-upload-pack and git-receive-pack
Manage Git references and refs with atomic operations
Extended commit handling with Ed25519 cryptographic signatures
author: AbdelStark
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. Ed25519 is strong modern crypto (misclassified as weak). Backticks are markdown code fences, not shell execution. Git protocol operations are legitimate repository functionality. No malicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: This skill provides implementation patterns for Git repository operations using the pure-Rust gitoxide library. It enables safe and efficient Git operations without external git binary dependencies.
limitations: This skill provides implementation patterns and documentation, not executable commands
Does not replace git CLI or provide direct git command execution
Requires gitoxide library integration into a Rust project
Not a standalone tool for end users

useCases.targetUser: Rust developers building version control tools
useCases.title: Build Git-powered Applications
useCases.description: Developers can use these patterns to build applications that interact with Git repositories using the pure-Rust gitoxide library instead of invoking the git CLI.

useCases.title: Implement Git Hosting Services
useCases.description: Create Git server functionality with custom protocol handlers for git-upload-pack and git-receive-pack operations.

useCases.targetUser: Security-conscious teams requiring cryptographic commit verification
useCases.title: Extend Git with Cryptographic Verification
useCases.description: Add Ed25519 signature verification to Git commits for enhanced integrity and authentication in custom Git workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Open a Repository
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I open an existing Git repository or create a new one using gitoxide in Rust? Show me the code pattern.
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to work with a Git repository programmatically

promptTemplates.title: Read Git Objects
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Rust code to read Git objects (commits, trees, blobs) using gitoxide and decode their contents.
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to inspect repository history or file contents

promptTemplates.title: Implement Git HTTP Protocol
promptTemplates.prompt: Create HTTP endpoints for git-upload-pack and git-receive-pack using axum and gitoxide for a Git server.

promptTemplates.title: Generate Packfiles
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to generate Git packfiles containing specific objects using gitoxide for efficient data transfer.
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to implement Git clone or fetch functionality

bestPractices: Use gitoxide's high-level gix crate for common Git operations instead of spawning git processes
Implement atomic reference updates using PreviousValue::MustExistAndMatch for safe concurrent operations
Verify Ed25519 signatures on commits when using cryptographic extensions for integrity
antiPatterns: Do not invoke git CLI commands when gitoxide provides equivalent functionality
Do not skip reference verification when accepting pushes
Do not expose raw packfile generation without proper authentication

faq.question: What is gitoxide?
faq.answer: Gitoxide is a pure-Rust implementation of Git that provides safe, efficient Git operations without depending on the git binary.

faq.question: Can this skill run git commands directly?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides implementation patterns and code examples for using gitoxide programmatically. It does not execute git commands.

faq.question: What is Ed25519 used for in this skill?
faq.answer: Ed25519 provides cryptographic signatures for commits, enabling verification of commit authenticity and integrity beyond standard Git hashes.

faq.question: What is the Smart HTTP protocol?
faq.answer: Smart HTTP is a Git protocol that uses HTTP POST requests for efficient clone, fetch, and push operations between clients and servers.

faq.question: How does packfile generation work?
faq.answer: Packfiles bundle Git objects efficiently for transfer. Gitoxide can generate packfiles containing specific objects based on want/have negotiation.

faq.question: Is this skill production-ready?
faq.answer: This skill provides patterns and examples. Production use requires proper integration, testing, and security review of the complete implementation.
